title: Clover Food Lab puts itself on the market
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Clover Food Lab is seeking a buyer after stabilizing post-bankruptcy and filed a notice for potential staff layoffs.
The company operates 11 restaurants, a meal box delivery service, and a catering business but no longer has food trucks.
Clover emerged from 2023 bankruptcy with slight store reductions, planning to expand by 50 locations in New England over five years.

title: ‘BPS transportation is failing us’: School bus delays have Boston parents calling for change
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston Public Schools' bus system faces criticism for failing to meet on-time benchmarks, affecting thousands of students daily.
Boston's school transport serves 19,000 students across 200 schools and has a $200 million budget, but still struggles with delays.
Parents report frequent cancellations and delays, forcing them to find alternative transport, impacting students' stability and learning.

title: How did the ‘Seger bar’ guy get Gov. Healey for his latest video?
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey appeared in Brian Daly's viral video series where bars are rated by playing Bob Seger music.
Healey and Daly filmed the video at 21st Amendment in Beacon Hill, certifying it as a 'Seger bar.'
Daly coincidentally met Healey while filming, leading to her cameo in the popular video series.

title: Michelin-recommended dumpling chain Nan Xiang to open in Framingham
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Nan Xiang Soup Dumplings will open its first Massachusetts location at 84 Worcester Rd., Framingham, later this year.
Known for handmade soup dumplings, the chain is part of Nan Xiang Long Bao, recommended by Michelin from 2009 to 2017.
The new location takes over the former Fresh Market space, adding to locations in Boston's Chinatown and Shrewsbury.

title: ‘The Drama’ review: Zendaya and Robert Pattinson’s darkly funny Boston movie is designed to discomfort
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: 'The Drama,' featuring Zendaya and Robert Pattinson, is a dark comedy set in Boston exploring social norms.
Director Kristoffer Borgli tackles taboo subjects and societal hypocrisy with humor and audacity in this A24 film.
The movie focuses on the couple's relationship turmoil leading to their wedding, probing themes of confession and redemption.

title: National UMass survey has bad news for Trump
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: UMass Amherst poll shows Trump's approval rating has dropped to 33%, the lowest of his second term.
A 2026 poll details falling support among Trump's key voter blocs, including men, working-class Americans, and Black voters.
Trump faces criticism for handling foreign policy and immigration; majority disapprove of his approach to inflation and jobs.

title: Globe Magazine names New England’s 50 fastest-growing companies
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Globe Magazine, with Statista, named New England's 50 fastest-growing companies, highlighting resilience in a challenging economy.
Companies faced economic challenges like inflation and labor shortages but achieved significant organic growth from 2021 to 2024.
Top companies include Tenovi, Salient, and HighByte, with industries ranging from IT to hospitality and custom manufacturing.

title: Judge denies Lindsay Clancy’s request for two-part murder trial
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Judge Sullivan denied Lindsay Clancy's request for a two-part trial in her murder case.
Clancy's lawyer proposed splitting the trial to assess the killings and her mental state separately.
Clancy is accused of killing her three children; trial is set for July 20, 2026.

title: What we know about the armed robbery of a massive Beverly mansion
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: An armed intruder broke into the 'Rock Edge' mansion in Beverly and assaulted the caretaker with a pistol.
The suspect stole several valuables and fled in a stolen Porsche, later recovered by police; the suspect remains at large.
Owned by Thomas J. Swan III, the historic mansion was sold for $18.275 million in 2023, the highest in Beverly history.

title: Walmart closes Worcester fulfillment center, laying off 90 employees
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Walmart closed its Sam's Club Fulfillment Center in Worcester, affecting 90 employees as part of fulfillment network consolidation.
Laid-off employees have a 90-day transition period to apply for other positions within Walmart and Sam's Club locations.
Eligible employees receive a $7,500 transfer incentive and potential relocation benefits for moves over 50 miles.
